audit_status.source_base: Greek James text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact draft reviewed for teachers, tongue, blessing/cursing, wisdom from above, peace-making.

verses.restored: Let not many become teachers, my siblings, knowing that we will receive greater judgment.
For we all stumble in many ways. If anyone does not stumble in word, this one is a complete human, able also to bridle the whole body.
If we put bits into horses' mouths so they obey us, we also turn about their whole body.
Look also at ships, being so great and driven by hard winds, yet turned by a very small rudder wherever the impulse of the pilot wills.
So also the tongue is a small member and boasts great things. Look how small a fire kindles how large a forest.
And the tongue is fire, the world of injustice among our members; it stains the whole body and sets the wheel of birth aflame, being itself set aflame by Gehenna.
For every nature of beasts and birds, reptiles and sea creatures, is tamed and has been tamed by human nature,
but the tongue no human is able to tame, a restless harmful thing, full of death-bringing poison.
With it we bless the Lord and Father, and with it we curse humans who have come to be according to likeness of Divine.
From the same mouth comes blessing and curse. My siblings, these things must not be so.
Does a spring pour forth from the same opening the sweet and the bitter?
Can a fig tree, my siblings, make olives, or a vine figs? Neither can salt water make sweet.
Who is wise and understanding among you? Let one show by beautiful conduct one's works in gentleness of wisdom.
But if you have bitter jealousy and selfish ambition in your heart, do not boast and lie against the truth.
This wisdom is not coming down from above, but earthly, soul-level, disruptive.
For where jealousy and selfish ambition are, there is disorder and every harmful practice.
But the wisdom from above is first pure, then peaceable, gentle, open to reason, full of mercy and beautiful/good fruits, impartial, unhypocritical.
And fruit of righteousness/justice is sown in peace by those making peace.

translation_notes: Speech ethics are central: humans bear Divine likeness and must not be cursed.
Wisdom from above is measurable by peace, gentleness, mercy, impartiality, and good fruit.
Gehenna is retained as Jewish apocalyptic image rather than flattened into later hell systems.
